zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton於1492年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾鮑頓時，她的父親John Sutton25歲，母親Isabella Puresey20歲。她於大約1512年與Sir Walter Blount在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾阿克頓 白 德洛特維其結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1562年1月8日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾武托希特享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Acton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, especially in Shropshire and adjacent counties, named Acton. Generally, these are from Old English āc ‘oak’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In Ireland the surname has been established in Connacht since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton was born in 1492, in Broughton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Sutton, was 25 and her mother, Isabella Puresey, was 20. She married Sir Walter Blount about 1512, in Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 9 daughters. She died on 8 January 1562, in Uttoxeter, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton est née en 1492, à Broughton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Sutton, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isabella Puresey, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Walter Blount environ 1512, à Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 janvier 1562, à Uttoxeter, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.StJohn the Baptist Church, Broughton, La: イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアブロウトン
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Actonが1492年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアブロウトンで生まれた時，父John Suttonは25歳，母Isabella Pureseyは20歳であった。約1512年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアアクトン ビー ドロイトウィチでSir Walter Blountと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は1562年1月8日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアウットオクセターで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Acton, Worcestershire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 악턴 비 드로이트위치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton(이)가 1492년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 브루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Sutton(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Puresey(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 악턴 비 드로이트위치에서 Sir Walter Blount(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1562년 1월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 우토세터에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.Acton, Worcestershire, England: Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Broughton, Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshir: Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton nasceu em 1492, em Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Sutton, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Puresey, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Walter Blount aproximadamente 1512, em Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 9 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de janeiro de 1562, em Uttoxeter, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Acton, Worcestershire, England: актон би дроитвич, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton родилась в 1492 г. в местности броугтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Sutton было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Puresey, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Walter Blount около 1512 г. в местности актон би дроитвич, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 9 дочерей. Она умерла 8 января 1562 г. в местности уттоксетир, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Acton, Worcestershire, England: Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Broughton, Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshir: Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Margaret Isabel Acton nació en 1492, en Broughton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Sutton, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Isabella Puresey, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Walter Blount aproximadamente 1512, en Acton by Droitwich, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 8 de enero de 1562, en Uttoxeter, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
